user.title: Zero Net Export
user.description: Create the Zero Net Export backend entry first, then finish Sensors/source roles, Managed Devices onboarding, Controls policy/live mode, and Diagnostics from Settings -> Devices & Services -> Integrations -> Zero Net Export -> Configure. Runtime defaults start in a safe bootstrap state until required source roles and managed devices are ready.

data_description.name: This is just the label Home Assistant will show for this Zero Net Export system. Pick something human-friendly, like the site, inverter, or switchboard name. After entry creation, continue in Configure for Sensors/source roles, Managed Devices onboarding, Controls policy/live mode, and Diagnostics from Settings -> Devices & Services -> Integrations -> Zero Net Export -> Configure.

setup_incomplete.title: Finish native Zero Net Export setup

device_inventory_invalid.title: Managed-device configuration needs repair

runtime_attention.title: Zero Net Export runtime blockers

init.title: Zero Net Export command center

native_setup.title: Sensors

data_description.grid_sensor_mode: Choose Combined when your hardware exposes one signed/net grid sensor. Choose Separate when Home Assistant already has dedicated import and export entities.

native_setup_sources.title: Sensors

data.grid_energy_entity_manual: Combined / net grid energy entity ID fallback (optional)
data.battery_soc_entity_manual: Battery state of charge entity ID fallback (optional)

data_description.grid_power_entity: Single signed/net grid power sensor. Positive values are treated as import and negative values are treated as export.
data_description.grid_energy_entity: Single signed/net grid energy sensor for the combined-grid energy source role. Positive values are treated as import and negative values are treated as export.
data_description.grid_energy_entity_manual: Optional native fallback if Home Assistant still rejects a valid Combined / net grid energy choice. Capture the exact error or a screenshot first, then clear the selector, paste the same energy entity ID into this fallback field, and save again.
data_description.home_load_power_entity: Optional whole-home load power sensor. If omitted, Zero Net Export infers household load from solar, grid, and battery flows when possible.
data_description.battery_soc_entity: Optional battery SOC sensor for reserve-aware control and battery reserve source health.
data_description.battery_soc_entity_manual: Optional native fallback if Home Assistant still rejects a valid Battery state of charge choice. Capture the exact error or a screenshot first, then clear the selector, paste the same battery SOC entity ID into this fallback field, and save again.

devices.title: Managed Devices

data_description.device_action: Choose whether to promote a surfaced fixed-load or variable-load candidate when one fits, add a device manually when no surfaced candidate is right, open the Managed Devices workspace to review enablement, edit device settings, or remove a device from the fleet, or use the advanced JSON editor only for recovery or bulk managed-device repair.

device_bulk_enable.title: Review managed devices workspace and enablement

device_edit_pick.title: Edit managed device

device_pick_candidate.title: Promotion shortlist

device_pick_candidate_full.title: Promotion full list

device_vetting.title: Promotion review

device_template.title: Promotion preset

device_add.title: {device_mode} managed device

data_description.entity_id: Choose the Home Assistant entity that directly controls the real load. Use the manual add path in the Managed Devices workspace only when the shortlist or full surfaced list did not already surface the right entity, and review the fit and warnings carefully before saving.

device_remove.title: Remove managed device

devices_json.title: Managed Devices JSON editor

data_description.device_inventory_json: Advanced recovery path. Paste a JSON array of controllable devices into this recovery field when you need bulk edits or to repair invalid managed-device configuration.

policy.title: Controls

data_description.target_export_w: The export value the controller aims for during normal operation. Leave this at 0 W unless you intentionally want a small export or import bias.
data_description.deadband_w: How much error Zero Net Export will tolerate before changing devices. Higher values reduce device flapping; lower values chase the target more aggressively.
data_description.battery_reserve_soc: The minimum battery percentage to protect before discretionary surplus-absorption actions are allowed. Choose the lowest battery level you still want to keep in reserve.
data_description.refresh_seconds: How often the backend re-reads your source sensors and re-plans control actions. Shorter intervals react faster; longer intervals reduce churn.

support.title: Diagnostics

advanced.title: Advanced JSON editor and recovery
advanced.description: Use the raw JSON editor only for advanced recovery or bulk managed-device repair, not for normal device onboarding.

error.device_inventory_invalid: The managed-device configuration is invalid or incomplete.
error.source_entities_invalid: One or more source entities are missing, duplicated, or invalid.
